title: Entropy as a secret-detection signal
description: Random credentials have high Shannon entropy, so measuring the randomness of a string flags likely secrets that no fixed pattern would match.
body: Use when reasoning about how scanners find unknown token formats. Do not expect pattern rules alone to catch every secret. High-entropy strings such as random base64 or hex tokens score far above ordinary text, which lets entropy thresholds surface secrets that match no provider pattern; the trade-off is false positives on hashes and ids. Inspect flagged strings to judge them without echoing the value. Validation signals include a random token being flagged while prose is not. Risk boundary: tuning entropy thresholds too high to cut noise can let real tokens through, so balance carefully and verify rather than dismiss.
